config.seed_text: A research vessel recovered a transmission from the trench floor. It began as engineering telemetry and slowly turned into philosophy.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.output_text: When all that work stopped there would soon emerge one important thing to note about its history: everything did not end on this point but rather came down through an interminable loop around "The" (the very first word) . But what is happening now does nothing for anyone involved; everyone continues making assumptions until they hit their ultimate destination…and then finally disappears again within minutes! At any rate we've got another new discovery here at home – which really should come straight out of no where near you or anywhere else.. And hey presto!! We know our mission accomplished right? Right...what do I need some help deciphering???!

TEXT:
A research vessel recovered a transmission from the trench floor. It began as engineering telemetry and slowly turned into philosophy. When all that work stopped there would soon emerge one important thing to note about its history: everything did not end on this point but rather came down through an interminable loop around "The" (the very first word) . But what is happening now does nothing for anyone involved; everyone continues making assumptions until they hit their ultimate destination…and then finally disappears again within minutes! At any rate we've got another new discovery here at home – which really should come straight out of no where near you or anywhere else.. And hey presto!! We know our mission accomplished right? Right...what do I need some help deciphering???!

records.output_text: There's always been something lurking behind those lines … and oh boy....so why don't i just go over them ? You'll notice how much more familiar every single piece of evidence looks like when your brain reads as 'yes' than while reading/reading ('no'). Not only has most words sound totally different from each other after being put together using ordinary writing techniques , yet many may actually seem completely similar during translation too — with seemingly impossible results due solely entirely coincidental differences made even worse upon inspection & analysis ! With such simple explanations let's see who wrote exactly 1% of English characters before 3 years old ... so maybe thats someone taking
